title: IGNCA and Asia-Pacific Register
focus: Focuses on the specific role of the Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts and India's entries in the regional Asia-Pacific register.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 012]
Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts (IGNCA)
- Premier government-funded arts organization in India. It is an autonomous institute under the Ministry of Culture, established in 1985.
- India’s engagement is led by the Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts (IGNCA), designated the Nodal Centre since 2014.
- IGNCA facilitates nominations, organizes awareness campaigns, conserves rare manuscripts, and conducts workshops on archival management.
- India has also made significant contributions to the Asia-Pacific Regional Register. These include:
- The illustrated Rāmacaritamānasa
- The Sahṛdayāloka-Locana (a Sanskrit poetics text)
- A rare 15th-century Pañcatantra manuscript.
- These inscriptions, first made in 2023, mark India’s growing regional participation and dedication to preserving storytelling, literary, and aesthetic traditions.
